raw.colM_extended: *Final amount will depend on time spent troubleshooting unresponsive chargers

raw.colG_description:
This proposal covers commissioning of Tesla chargers only. Excluded from our scope are the supply of chargers,
pedestals, or software; transformer or main panel upgrades, feeders, fuses, or ADA measures; design, engineering,
permitting, and associated fees or coordination; civil work such as trenching, boring, drilling, concrete/asphalt work,
pads, bollards, curbs, or striping; conduit and conductor installation, sub-panels, mounting hardware, or other electrical infrastructure; networking or connectivity equipment and installation; and site logistics such as equipment rental, fencing, trench plates, or rock/masonry removal. Troubleshooting, if required, will be billed separately on a time and materials basis.

Troubleshooting rates only apply where Tesla chargers are not functioning due to installation or electrical issues
performed by others. Our role is to diagnose the problem, prepare a written report, and provide it to the owner so
corrective action can be taken by their electrician.

Tesla chargers also require Wi-Fi connectivity to operate properly. If issues are related to Wi-Fi or network outages, coordination with the property’s technology department will be necessary, as Bullet EV cannot resolve connectivity-related problems.

Tesla Universal Wall Connector Connectivity Guidelines

raw.colE_qty:
Change Orders:
All changes to the original scope of work must be submitted in writing and approved by both parties through a signed Change Order. Approved changes may affect project cost, timeline, or scope. No additional work will be performed without written authorization via a Change Order.
raw.colG_description:
Cancellation Policy:
Client understands that Bullet EV incurs costs immediately upon project engagement, including but not limited to site evaluation, design review, and permitting preparation. If the Client cancels the project after the site evaluation has been completed, the initial deposit shall be non-refundable and retained to cover administrative and technical expenses. Cancellations before the site evaluation may be eligible for a partial refund, less incurred costs.

This proposal is for equipment purchase and delivery only. Pricing includes procurement of the listed chargers and related equipment and delivery to the project site only. This proposal does not include installation, commissioning, programming, configuration, activation, permitting, utility coordination, site preparation, electrical work, trenching, concrete, mounting, network setup, freight beyond standard delivery, unloading, storage, warranty labor, maintenance, service, troubleshooting, or any other labor or onsite services unless specifically stated otherwise in this proposal.

raw.colG_description:
Cancellation Policy:
Client understands that Bullet EV incurs costs immediately upon project engagement, including but not limited to site evaluation, design review, and permitting preparation. If the Client cancels the project after the site evaluation has been completed, the initial deposit shall be non-refundable and retained to cover administrative and technical expenses. Cancellations before the site evaluation may be eligible for a partial refund, less incurred costs.
Base Design/Eng-3-4 sheet, site plan, riser, 1-line, CD50-CD100
Permit Coordination and Filing-Up to 2 visits in person
Utility Coordination-Up to 2 visits in person
Furnish & Install EMT Conduit-Up to 2" w/ up to #4 Conductors

The following items are not included in this proposal unless specifically noted otherwise. Excluded scope includes utility owned equipment and upgrades such as transformers and related utility upgrades, main panel upgrades, main feeds, fuses, and any required ADA compliance measures as determined by the Authority Having Jurisdiction or the site owner. This proposal excludes all design, engineering, and permitting services, including site walks, load studies, engineered or stamped plan sets, as built drawings, permit fees, and extended utility or permitting coordination beyond two in person visits. All civil scope is excluded, including trenching, boring, core drilling, concrete or asphalt removal and restoration, curb or foundation work, transformer pad preparation, concrete bases for pedestals, conduit encasement, striping, signage, stenciling, wheel stops, and bollard installation. Electrical infrastructure beyond the charger and pedestal installation scope is excluded, including conduit and conductor installation, sub panels, balance of system materials, and mounting hardware not expressly listed. Networking and connectivity equipment, including Wi Fi, extenders, and network setup, are excluded and must be provided by the owner. Site logistics and safety items such as equipment rental, construction fencing, traffic control beyond blocking designated parking spaces, rock or masonry removal, irrigation repair, off site staging, and after hours work are also excluded.

Please refer to the following document for Tesla Wi Fi guidance and connectivity requirements. Tesla chargers require a reliable Wi Fi connection for proper operation and full functionality. If Wi Fi is not available at the installation site, Bullet Energy Solutions can provide a separate proposal for the networking infrastructure needed to support charger connectivity.

Tesla Universal Wall Connector Connectivity Guidelines

This proposal does not include transformer procurement or utility upgrades, main panel upgrades or modifications to main service feeds or fusing, ADA compliance measures as required by the AHJ or owner, off-site staging or laydown areas, after-hours work outside of scheduled shutdown coordination, or traffic control beyond blocking designated parking spaces. All civil work is excluded, including trenching, boring, core drilling, concrete or asphalt removal and restoration, curb or foundation work, transformer or vault pads, pedestal concrete bases, conduit encasement, striping, signage, wheel stops, bollards, and ground preparation. Electrical infrastructure beyond the listed scope is excluded, including conduit, conductors, sub-panels, and networking or connectivity equipment. Site logistics and safety items such as equipment rentals, fencing, trench plates, rock or masonry removal, and irrigation repair are also excluded unless specifically noted otherwise.

raw.colG_description:
Cancellation Policy:
Client understands that Bullet EV incurs costs immediately upon project engagement, including but not limited to site evaluation, design review, and permitting preparation. If the Client cancels the project after the site evaluation has been completed, the initial deposit shall be non-refundable and retained to cover administrative and technical expenses. Cancellations before the site evaluation may be eligible for a partial refund, less incurred costs.
Level 2 EV Charger Installation (Customer Supplied Charger)
Permitting Documentation and Submittal (Includes One Line Diagram)

Includes installation of a dedicated circuit breaker in the main garage electrical panel and installation of the EV charger within 15 feet of the panel. Additional distance or scope beyond 15 feet will require a change order.

Tesla chargers require a reliable Wi-Fi connection to operate and enable full functionality. If Wi-Fi is not available at the installation site, Bullet Energy Solutions can provide a separate proposal for the installation of the necessary networking infrastructure to support charger connectivity.

Exclusions: EV charger (customer supplied); wall or ceiling patching and paint; concrete, stucco, or masonry repair; panel or service upgrades; trenching or restoration; concealed wiring or wall fishing; conduit or wire runs beyond 15 feet from the main garage panel; and any additional requirements or fees required by the AHJ or utility. Any excluded or additional scope requires a change order.
